name: Current Affairs in Indian Polity (2): Recent Constitutional Amendment Bills

sourceRefs.label: PRS India/Wikipedia - 130th Amendment Bill 2025 (removal of detained ministers); 131st Amendment Bill 2026 (Lok Sabha delimitation); PIB - Bills passed in 2025

researchNotes: Second Polity current-affairs topic (2nd lap). Both bills are recent, specifically dated, and structurally significant — directly extending this project's existing Union Executive (Day 33) and Union Legislature content with proposed (not-yet-finalised) changes, clearly flagged as BILLS under consideration/scrutiny rather than enacted law. Expansion pass (Batch 13): CRITICAL UPDATE — the 131st Amendment Bill (described in the original content as merely 'introduced') was subsequently DEFEATED in the Lok Sabha on 17 April 2026; without this update the topic would misleadingly imply the bill was still a live proposal. Also added the Women's Reservation Act's gazette notification (with the reservation itself still inoperative) and the One Nation One Election bill's JPC status.

reading.intro: Covers two major recent Constitutional Amendment Bills — one on removing detained ministers, another on Lok Sabha delimitation and expansion — both still in the legislative process, plus overall Parliament activity statistics for 2025.

qa.question: What does the Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill, 2025 propose, and when was it introduced?
qa.answer: Introduced in the Lok Sabha on 20 August 2025. It proposes the REMOVAL of the Prime Minister, Chief Ministers, and other Ministers if they are arrested and remain in detention for 30 CONSECUTIVE DAYS on charges punishable by imprisonment of 5 years or more. It has been referred to a Joint Committee of both Houses for detailed scrutiny (i.e., not yet enacted).

qa.question: What does the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026 propose, and what other bills were introduced alongside it?
qa.answer: Introduced on 16 April 2026, alongside the Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026 and the Delimitation Bill, 2026. Together, they seek to enable Lok Sabha delimitation based on the 2011 Census, increase the maximum Lok Sabha membership to 850 (up to 815 from states, up to 35 from Union Territories), and enable women's reservation in the Lok Sabha to be based on this new delimitation.

qa.question: As of March 2026, how many times has the Constitution of India been amended in total?
qa.answer: 106 amendments since the Constitution was first enacted in 1950.

qa.question: How many Bills did Parliament pass during the calendar year 2025, and what is one notable example?
qa.answer: 39 Bills were passed by both Houses during January-December 2025. A notable example: the Waqf (Amendment) Bill, 2025, aimed at improving management, stakeholder empowerment, and survey/registration/case-disposal efficiency for waqf properties.

qa.question: IMPORTANT UPDATE: What happened to the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026 when it came to a vote in the Lok Sabha?
qa.answer: It was DEFEATED in the Lok Sabha on 17 April 2026 — receiving 298 votes in favour and 230 against, short of the required two-thirds majority (352 of the 528 members voting) needed for a constitutional amendment. This means the proposed delimitation/Lok Sabha-expansion changes described above did NOT become law — an important correction, since the bill's mere introduction (April 2026) does not mean it passed.

qa.question: What happened to the Women's Reservation Act (106th Constitutional Amendment) on 16 April 2026, and what remains pending?
qa.answer: The Union Ministry of Law and Justice issued a gazette notification bringing the Act formally into force on 16 April 2026 (originally passed by Parliament in September 2023). However, the actual 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies remains INOPERATIVE — it is conditional on the first Census taken after this notification, followed by a fresh delimitation exercise, neither of which has happened yet.

qa.question: What is the 'One Nation, One Election' proposal, and what is its current legislative status?
qa.answer: A proposal to synchronise Lok Sabha and all State Assembly elections. The Constitution (129th Amendment) Bill, 2024 was introduced in the Lok Sabha on 17 December 2024 and referred to a 39-member Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) chaired by MP P.P. Chaudhary. As of the latest updates, it remains under JPC review — not yet passed, with its eventual fate still uncertain.

options: It passed with the required two-thirds majority

questions.explanation: Defeated — 298 in favour, 230 against, short of the required 352.
questions.trick: Always distinguish a bill's INTRODUCTION date from its final VOTE outcome — current-affairs questions often test whether a proposal actually became law or was rejected/is still pending.

questions.prompt: The Women's Reservation Act (106th Amendment) was gazette-notified into force on 16 April 2026. What does this notification NOT yet achieve?
options: Nothing — the 33% reservation is immediately active
The actual 33% reservation, which awaits a fresh Census and delimitation
Reservation only in the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: The 33% reservation remains inoperative pending Census + delimitation.

questions.prompt: The Constitution (129th Amendment) Bill, 2024, related to 'One Nation, One Election', is currently:
options: Under review by a Joint Parliamentary Committee
Struck down by the Supreme Court
